title: US-002: Phase 1 — SSH key check and generation
description:
Implement check_or_generate_key() and check_key_permissions() functions in the new script.

## Context
- If ~/.ssh/id_ed25519 exists: skip generation, print skip message
- If missing: generate with ssh-keygen -t ed25519 -C "$MACHINE_NAME"
- Passphrase injection: write a temp SSH_ASKPASS script to $(mktemp), chmod 700, use it for ssh-keygen, delete immediately; add trap for cleanup
- If $sshEntryName is empty (no KeePassXC entry): run ssh-keygen interactively without SSH_ASKPASS
- After generation: print reminder to store the key in KeePassXC
- check_key_permissions(): warn if id_ed25519 is not chmod 600 (non-fatal, use print_message "warning")

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] check_or_generate_key() skips if ~/.ssh/id_ed25519 exists
- [ ] Generates ed25519 key with machine name as -C comment when key is missing
- [ ] Passphrase sourced from KeePassXC via SSH_ASKPASS temp script (when $sshEntryName non-empty)
- [ ] Falls back to interactive ssh-keygen when $sshEntryName is empty
- [ ] trap ensures temp SSH_ASKPASS file is deleted on exit/error
- [ ] Reminder printed after generation to store key in KeePassXC
- [ ] check_key_permissions() warns on wrong permissions (non-fatal)
- [ ] chezmoi execute-template expands the script without errors

title: US-001: Script scaffolding and template preamble
description:
Create the new chezmoi script file with all boilerplate in place, ready for phase implementations.

## Context
- File: home/.chezmoiscripts/run_onchange_after_darwin-46-setup-ssh-github.sh.tmpl
- Position 46 runs after GitHub auth (45), before system config (80+)
- Refer to home/.chezmoiscripts/run_onchange_after_darwin-45-setup-github-auth.sh.tmpl for the pattern to follow
- Refer to home/.chezmoiscripts/run_onchange_after_darwin-97-test-ssh-github.sh.tmpl for the hash-trigger pattern

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] File created at correct path with correct chezmoi naming convention
- [ ] Platform guard: {{- if and (eq .chezmoi.os "darwin") .has_keepassxc_db -}}
- [ ] set -euo pipefail
- [ ] Template preamble resolves machine-settings, extracts $sshEntryName (keepassxc_entries.ssh), bakes in MACHINE_NAME via machine-name template, bakes in SSH_PASSPHRASE from (keepassxc $sshEntryName).Password (guarded: only if $sshEntryName is non-empty)
- [ ] run_onchange trigger comment hashes ~/.ssh/id_ed25519.pub using sha256sum
- [ ] Sources scripts/shared-utils.sh
- [ ] Calls require_tools gh ssh-keygen ssh-add at script start
- [ ] main() function stub that calls placeholder phase functions
- [ ] Script closes with {{ end -}}

title: US-005: Phase 5 — GitHub SSH connectivity test
description:
Port and integrate the connectivity test from the retiring script-97 into the new script.

## Context
- Source to port from: home/.chezmoiscripts/run_onchange_after_darwin-97-test-ssh-github.sh.tmpl
- Functions to port: test_ssh_connection(), analyze_ssh_output(), print_permission_denied_help(), print_network_error_help(), print_unknown_error_help()
- GitHub exits with code 1 on successful auth (no shell access) — treat exit 1 + "successfully authenticated" in output as success
- Timeout: use gtimeout (macOS coreutils) or timeout; handle exit codes 124/142 as timeout
- SSH flags: -T -o ConnectTimeout=5 -o BatchMode=yes git@github.com
- Remove the emoji (📚) from the help text to match shared-utils.sh print_message style

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] test_ssh_connection() uses timeout with fallback (gtimeout → timeout → no timeout)
- [ ] analyze_ssh_output() treats exit 1 + "successfully authenticated" as success
- [ ] Timeout exit codes (124, 142) produce a clear timeout error message
- [ ] print_permission_denied_help() gives step-by-step remediation
- [ ] print_network_error_help() covers DNS, firewall, VPN causes
- [ ] print_unknown_error_help() suggests checking GitHub status
- [ ] main() calls test_ssh_connection() last; exits 0 on success, 1 on failure
- [ ] chezmoi execute-template expands the script without errors

title: US-004: Phases 3 & 4 — Register auth and signing keys on GitHub
description:
Implement GitHub SSH key registration with idempotency via fingerprint comparison.

## Context
- get_local_fingerprint(): ssh-keygen -lf ~/.ssh/id_ed25519.pub | awk '{print $2}'
- is_key_registered TYPE: call gh ssh-key list --json key,type, compute fingerprint for each returned key (ssh-keygen -lf), compare against local fingerprint for given type
- register_github_key TYPE: call is_key_registered; if not found, call gh ssh-key add ~/.ssh/id_ed25519.pub --type "$1" --title "MACHINE_NAME" (MACHINE_NAME is the baked-in value from template)
- main() calls: register_github_key authentication, then register_github_key signing
- Both use the same ~/.ssh/id_ed25519.pub file

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] get_local_fingerprint() returns SHA256 fingerprint of local public key
- [ ] is_key_registered() correctly identifies already-registered keys by fingerprint
- [ ] register_github_key() skips with print_message "skip" if already registered
- [ ] register_github_key() calls gh ssh-key add with correct --type and --title flags
- [ ] Both authentication and signing types are registered in main()
- [ ] Machine name is the baked-in template value (not a runtime variable)
- [ ] chezmoi execute-template expands the script without errors

title: US-003: Phase 2 — SSH agent loading with macOS Keychain
description:
Implement load_ssh_agent() to load the key into the SSH agent idempotently using macOS Keychain.

## Context
- ~/.ssh/config already has UseKeychain yes / AddKeysToAgent yes for github.com
- Fingerprint check: ssh-keygen -lf ~/.ssh/id_ed25519.pub | awk '{print $2}' vs ssh-add -l
- If already loaded: skip with print_message "skip"
- If not loaded: run ssh-add --apple-use-keychain ~/.ssh/id_ed25519 with SSH_ASKPASS temp script (same pattern as US-002)
- Fallback: if --apple-use-keychain fails with "illegal option", retry with plain ssh-add ~/.ssh/id_ed25519
- No-passphrase case: run ssh-add without SSH_ASKPASS (macOS Keychain dialog appears once)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] load_ssh_agent() skips if key fingerprint already present in ssh-add -l
- [ ] Runs ssh-add --apple-use-keychain with SSH_ASKPASS when passphrase available
- [ ] Falls back to plain ssh-add if --apple-use-keychain is unsupported
- [ ] Falls back to interactive ssh-add (no SSH_ASKPASS) when no passphrase available
- [ ] Temp SSH_ASKPASS file deleted after use; trap ensures cleanup
- [ ] chezmoi execute-template expands the script without errors

title: SSH GitHub Setup Script
description:
Add run_onchange_after_darwin-46-setup-ssh-github.sh.tmpl to handle end-to-end SSH key setup for GitHub: key presence check/generation, SSH agent loading with macOS Keychain, authentication and signing key registration on GitHub, and connectivity verification. Retire the existing run_onchange_after_darwin-97-test-ssh-github.sh.tmpl (absorbed into the new script).

Source: openspec/changes/setup-ssh-github/

title: US-006: Validate new script and retire script-97
description:
Final validation of the new script and cleanup of the retired script.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] chezmoi execute-template < home/.chezmoiscripts/run_onchange_after_darwin-46-setup-ssh-github.sh.tmpl expands without errors and shows no raw {{ }} template markers
- [ ] Expanded output contains the resolved machine name (not a template placeholder)
- [ ] Expanded output does NOT contain the raw passphrase value in any position other than the SSH_PASSPHRASE variable assignment (security check)
- [ ] shellcheck on the expanded script output passes with no errors (warnings acceptable)
- [ ] chezmoi diff confirms the new 46 script is managed
- [ ] home/.chezmoiscripts/run_onchange_after_darwin-97-test-ssh-github.sh.tmpl is deleted
- [ ] chezmoi diff confirms script-97 is gone
- [ ] CLAUDE.md script ordering comment updated: replace "97: SSH test" with "46: SSH GitHub setup"
